sutta: the variable sariputa went to visit the venerable mauglana the great maha moghilana and on coming to him greeted him courteously and after the exchange of greetings and courtesy sat down at 1 side so seated the venerable sariputta said to the verbal mahamu glana moglana your reverence there are these 4 modes of progress what 4 the more the progress that is painful with sluggish higher intuition etc as before now your reverence by way of which of these 4 modes of progress was your heart released from the asavas without grasping sariputta your reverence there are as you say these 4 modes of progress now of these 4 the 1 i followed was the mode of progress that is painful but with swift higher intuition by way of this mode of progress was my mind released from the asavas without grasping that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this i purposely read this to let you know that maha mauglana 1 of the greatest disciples of the buddha in his cultivation of the uh holy path the holy life he was having a lot of uh uh he had an abundance of greed hatred and delusion lust great hatred and delusion and these uh hindered his progress his progress was painful he was continually bothered by these things but in spite of that now he he made a lot of effort and then he became an arahant so from this uh we get we can get inspiration we can get consolation to know that even somebody so great like maha mogalana his progress in the holy life was painful because of strong lust hatred and delusion so if we cultivate the holy life and we find sort of um how we feel and that practice of the holy life is so difficult and we uh we are not getting inspired then we should think of somebody like maha maglana if we follow his example and strive 1 day we'll also come to distinction and then suta

items: painful but with swift higher intuition
pleasant but with sluggish higher intuition
both pleasant and accompanied by swift higher intuition
